# throwaway

> Disposable & invalid email detector — bloom filter API on Cloudflare Workers

## What it does

Given an email address or domain, returns whether the TLD is a real ICANN-recognized suffix and whether the domain is a known disposable/temporary email provider (mailinator, guerrillamail, etc). Built on a 100k+ domain bloom filter for fast O(1) lookups.

Use this before sending signup emails, creating accounts, or accepting form submissions to block throwaway addresses and syntactically invalid domains.

## API

Base URL: `https://throwaway.sslboard.com`

### Check a single email

```
GET /check?email=user@example.com
```

```json
{
  "email": "user@mailinator.com",
  "domain": "mailinator.com",
  "valid_tld": true,
  "disposable": true
}
```

### Check a single domain

```
GET /check?domain=example.com
```

```json
{
  "domain": "example.com",
  "valid_tld": true,
  "disposable": false
}
```

### Batch check emails

```
POST /check
Content-Type: application/json

{ "emails": ["a@mailinator.com", "b@gmail.com", "c@fake.notarealtld"] }
```

```json
{
  "results": [
    { "email": "a@mailinator.com", "domain": "mailinator.com", "valid_tld": true, "disposable": true },
    { "email": "b@gmail.com", "domain": "gmail.com", "valid_tld": true, "disposable": false },
    { "email": "c@fake.notarealtld", "domain": "fake.notarealtld", "valid_tld": false, "disposable": false }
  ]
}
```

### Batch check domains

```
POST /check
Content-Type: application/json

{ "domains": ["mailinator.com", "yahoo.com"] }
```

```json
{
  "results": [
    { "domain": "mailinator.com", "valid_tld": true, "disposable": true },
    { "domain": "yahoo.com", "valid_tld": true, "disposable": false }
  ]
}
```

### Filter stats

```
GET /stats
```

```json
{
  "itemCount": 139000,
  "bitCount": 2000000,
  "hashCount": 10,
  "byteSize": 250000,
  "falsePositiveRate": 0.004
}
```

## Response fields

| Field | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `valid_tld` | boolean | `true` if the domain ends in a real ICANN-recognized TLD (`.com`, `.co.uk`, etc). `false` means the address can't possibly receive mail. |
| `disposable` | boolean | `true` if the domain is in the disposable-email blocklist. Only meaningful when `valid_tld` is `true`. |

## How to interpret results

1. **`valid_tld: false`** → reject. The domain is not real.
2. **`valid_tld: true`, `disposable: true`** → reject. Known throwaway provider.
3. **`valid_tld: true`, `disposable: false`** → accept. Looks legitimate.

No API key needed. No rate limits currently enforced.

## About

Built by the people at [SSLBoard](https://sslboard.com).
